21:30Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the feature

It is becoming increasingly common for scientists and others to run their analysis on cloud platforms or in containerized environments. Currently our documentation and testing focuses on users which are installing sunpy on their own machines using conda. That approach is not necessarily appropriate on on-demand cloud servers that usually want an installation as light and as quick as possible.

### Proposed solution

I would suggest that the following are needed to deal with this issue

- test on other operating systems such as aws linux or other specific linux flavors that are frequently used on the cloud or in containers
- Provide specific recommendations for base containers for users (e.g. docker files?)
- Update documentation with specific sections on how to install sunpy on linux systems without conda.

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6403/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6403/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/1243816924","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6403#issuecomment-1243816924","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6403","id":1243816924,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh85KIyPc","user":{"login":"dbarrous","id":19628176,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5NjI4MTc2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19628176?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous","html_url":"https://github.com/dbarrous","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-09-12T14:21:30Z","updated_at":"2022-09-12T14:21:30Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"A lot of the main cloud providers allow you to build serverless function containers for their specific platform (i.e. AWS -> Lambda Function), so I think adding tests that build the images and run some of the common functionality, or even just the unit tests that have already been created within a container with Sunpy (and other external dependencies) would be a good start. 

Here are some resources on how to build your own serverless container images for each of the most popular cloud providers:

- AWS Lambda (aws) - [https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/images-create.html](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/images-create.html)
- Google Cloud Functions (gcloud) - [https://cloud.google.com/functions/docs/building#image_registry_options](https://cloud.google.com/functions/docs/building#image_registry_options)
- Azure Functions (MS Azure) - [https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-create-function-linux-custom-image?tabs=in-process%2Cbash%2Cazure-cli&pivots=programming-language-csharp](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-create-function-linux-custom-image?tabs=in-process%2Cbash%2Cazure-cli&pivots=programming-language-csharp)

As @ehsteve suggested, we could create a docker file for each of the configurations. Then just add building + running the tests into the CI/CD pipeline to ensure Sunpy is not crashing the serverless function container when it is run.

Also, extra documentation on how to properly run Sunpy in a serverless environment would be beneficial as well (i.e. example config files or even just a walk-through of a simple implementation).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/1243816924/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6368","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6368/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6368/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6368/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6368","id":1342060830,"node_id":"I_kwDOACEKh85P_jke","number":6368,"title":"Issue when importing AppDirs in an AWS Lambda Function (Serverless Code)","user":{"login":"dbarrous","id":19628176,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5NjI4MTc2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19628176?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous","html_url":"https://github.com/dbarrous","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dbarrous/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-08-17T17:15:42Z","updated_at":"2022-08-17T17:15:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the bug

In a serverless (cloud) environment where user directories are not clearly defined, it's causing a read-only OSError that crashes the serverless function that is trying to run.
 
For example, we are trying to run Sunpy in an AWS Lambda environment, and the function `_is_writable_dir` in [sunpy.util.config](https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/_modules/sunpy/util/config.html) is causing the Lambda function to crash on import (`from sunpy.extern.appdirs import AppDirs`). 

Here is a related issue where we document the finding in our package: https://github.com/HERMES-SOC/hermes_core/issues/21

Here is a PR with a temporary fix that can provide more details on the issue as well: https://github.com/HERMES-SOC/hermes_core/pull/22 

### To Reproduce

(In an AWS Lambda Environment)
from sunpy.extern.appdirs import AppDirs
